title: Audio: Monotonic Stack — keep the unresolved in order

cues.label: The trigger
cues.headline: "Nearest bigger/smaller" looks quadratic
cues.narration: When each element needs the nearest larger or smaller neighbor, the brute force is a scan inside a scan; a monotonic stack makes it one pass.

cues.label: Unresolved in order
cues.headline: The stack holds only open answers
cues.narration: You keep on the stack only the elements whose answer is still open, always sorted increasing or decreasing.

cues.label: Pop resolves
cues.headline: A newcomer settles the accounts
cues.narration: When a new element breaks the order you pop, and each pop is the moment the popped element's answer becomes known.

cues.label: Amortized linear
cues.headline: Push once, pop at most once
cues.narration: Every element is pushed once and popped at most once, so the inner while loop is amortized constant and the scan is linear.

cues.label: Direction & indices
cues.headline: Increasing vs decreasing; store indices
cues.narration: Pick the direction from what arrival resolves a waiter; store indices so a pop yields both a value and a width.

cues.label: Leftovers
cues.headline: Default or flushing sentinel
cues.narration: Elements never popped keep a default answer, or you append a sentinel that forces every one to be measured in the same loop.

cues.label: When it fails
cues.headline: Only for nearest-monotonic-neighbor
cues.narration: If there is no waiting element resolved by a later arrival, the stack has nothing to do — reach for a heap or a sort instead.
